attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Research whether DeepSeek's recent performance improvements (late 2024 to early 2025) are related to adopting multi-agent or "agent swarm" architectures. Specifically:

1. Search for any official DeepSeek technical reports, blog posts, or papers mentioning multi-agent systems, mixture-of-agents, or agent collaboration in their training or inference pipeline.
2. Look for news articles or community discussions about DeepSeek R1, DeepSeek V3, or any recent DeepSeek model mentioning agent groups, parallel reasoning agents, or distributed agent inference.
3. Check if DeepSeek has published anything about using multiple specialized agents working together.

Search terms to try:
- "DeepSeek multi-agent architecture"
- "DeepSeek agent swarm"  
- "DeepSeek R1 V3 agent collaboration"
- "DeepSeek mixture of agents"
- site:arxiv.org deepseek multi-agent
- DeepSeek 2024 2025 agent group

Report what you find with source URLs. Be specific about which model versions are involved and what the actual mechanism is. If nothing concrete about agent groups is found, say so clearly and note what IS behind DeepSeek's improvements.

Research the current state of theory and practice around multi-agent task decomposition — specifically how to split complex tasks among multiple subagents working in parallel. The user wants to understand how to divide work effectively when spawning multiple concurrent subagents.

Search for:
1. Academic papers on multi-agent task decomposition (arxiv, Google Scholar)
   - Terms: "multi-agent task decomposition", "LLM agent orchestration", "task planning multi-agent", "hierarchical multi-agent systems"
2. Frameworks and practical implementations:
   - LangGraph multi-agent patterns
   - AutoGen task decomposition
   - CrewAI role-based decomposition
   - OpenAI Swarm / swarm agent patterns
   - MetaGPT role decomposition
3. Specific decomposition strategies/taxonomies:
   - How do researchers categorize task splitting? (functional decomposition, data decomposition, pipeline decomposition, etc.)
   - What heuristics exist for deciding what goes to which agent?
   - How are dependencies between subtasks managed?
4. Look for survey papers on LLM multi-agent systems from 2024-2025

For each approach/paper found, note:
- The decomposition strategy used
- How agents are assigned roles
- How results are aggregated
- Any formal taxonomy or classification scheme

Include arxiv links and framework documentation URLs. Focus on practical guidance, not just theoretical abstractions.

content.text:
I now have a thorough picture from primary sources. Here is a complete, fully sourced report.

---

## Research Findings: DeepSeek Performance Improvements and Multi-Agent / "Agent Swarm" Architecture

### Short Answer

There is no evidence — in any official DeepSeek technical report, paper, blog post, or credible news source — that DeepSeek's late-2024 to early-2025 performance improvements (in V3 or R1) are attributable to multi-agent architectures, "agent swarms," or multiple specialized agents collaborating during training or inference. The two core official papers (the DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report and the DeepSeek-R1 paper) make no mention of such mechanisms. The performance gains come from a distinct and well-documented set of single-model innovations described below.

---

### What Was Actually Searched

All six requested search angles were covered:

1. **"DeepSeek multi-agent architecture"** — returned results only about third-party frameworks (CrewAI, MetaGPT, IBM tutorials) using DeepSeek models as a component inside a multi-agent pipeline. Nothing from DeepSeek itself.
2. **"DeepSeek agent swarm"** — returned a single notable hit: Kimi K2.6 (a competitor, not DeepSeek) uses an "Agent Swarm" feature. DeepSeek did not appear in that context.
3. **"DeepSeek R1 V3 agent collaboration / parallel reasoning agents"** — no results about collaborative agents inside DeepSeek's training or inference stack.
4. **"DeepSeek mixture of agents"** — "Mixture of Experts" (MoE) repeatedly appeared, which is a neural network routing architecture, not a multi-agent collaboration pattern.
5. **site:arxiv.org deepseek multi-agent** — returned only the V3 and R1 primary papers, plus unrelated third-party papers using DeepSeek as a tool. Neither primary paper mentions multi-agent training or inference.
6. **"DeepSeek 2024 2025 agent group"** — the word "group" appears in DeepSeek's work only in the context of **GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization)**, an RL algorithm — not agent groups.

---

### What IS Behind DeepSeek's Improvements

The improvements across DeepSeek-V3 (December 2024) and DeepSeek-R1 (January 2025) come from four concrete, well-documented single-model mechanisms:

#### 1. Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Architecture — DeepSeek-V3
DeepSeek-V3 is a 671B-parameter MoE model where only 37B parameters are activated per token. This is an architectural efficiency feature — a sparse gating mechanism routes each token to the most relevant "experts" (sub-networks) within a single model. This is not "multiple agents"; it is a routing layer inside one model. Key innovations include:
- An **auxiliary-loss-free load balancing** strategy (novel vs. prior MoE models)
- **Multi-Token Prediction** training objective, predicting multiple future tokens simultaneously for stronger representation learning

#### 2. Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) — DeepSeek-V2 and V3
MLA compresses the Key-Value (KV) cache by projecting KV vectors into a low-rank latent space, expanding effective batch capacity approximately 9.6x over standard multi-head attention. This reduces memory overhead during inference dramatically, not through agent parallelism but through matrix compression.

#### 3. Hardware-Algorithm Co-Design — DeepSeek-V3
Three interlocking engineering innovations:
- **FP8 mixed-precision training**: validated at trillion-parameter scale for the first time; FP8 has double the FLOPS of FP16 on Nvidia Tensor Cores
- **DualPipe algorithm**: a novel pipeline parallelism scheduler that overlaps computation and communication, reducing idle "pipeline bubbles"
- **PTX-level GPU optimizations**: warp-level specialization for maximum H800 utilization

These reduced full training cost to 2.788M H800 GPU hours with no loss spikes across 14.8 trillion tokens.

#### 4. Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) — DeepSeek-R1
This is the key RL innovation for reasoning. GRPO:
- Generates a **group of multiple candidate outputs** for each prompt using the current policy
- Scores each output with a rule-based reward (correctness + format)
- Computes the **relative advantage** of each output within the group (group mean as the baseline), replacing the separate critic/value-function model used in PPO
- Updates the policy to favor outputs that scored above the group average

The word "group" here refers to a group of sampled outputs from a single model, not a group of separate agents. This is a within-model self-play technique. It produced emergent behaviors: self-reflection, backtracking ("aha moments"), and dynamic reasoning length — all from a single model trained with pure RL, requiring no human-labeled reasoning trajectories.

---

### Terminology Clarification: What "Agent" Means for DeepSeek

The confusion around "agents" and DeepSeek likely has two sources:

- **MoE as "experts"**: The word "experts" in Mixture-of-Experts sounds like agents, but these are sub-networks within one model, not autonomous agents.
- **DeepSeek's future roadmap**: Bloomberg reported in September 2025 that DeepSeek was preparing a dedicated AI agent product for late 2025, and DeepSeek-V3.1 (August 2025) was described as "the first step toward the agent era" — but this is about building agent-capable products on top of the model, not about using agents to train it.
- **Third-party use**: Many developers (AWS/SageMaker + CrewAI, IBM + MetaGPT) do build multi-agent systems using DeepSeek models as components, but this is external to DeepSeek's own training/inference architecture.

---

### Summary Table

| Claim | Evidence Status |
|---|---|
| DeepSeek uses multi-agent training | Not found in any official paper |
| DeepSeek uses "agent swarms" for inference | Not found — this was Kimi K2.6 (competitor) |
| DeepSeek V3 / R1 use agent collaboration | No evidence in V3 or R1 technical reports |
| DeepSeek publishes on mixture-of-agents | No official DeepSeek paper on this topic found |
| GRPO uses "groups" of agents | No — "group" = group of sampled outputs from one model |
| MoE uses multiple "expert" sub-networks | Yes, but these are layers in a single model, not agents |

---

Sources:
- [DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning (arXiv:2501.12948)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12948)
- [DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report (arXiv:2412.19437)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19437)
- [DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report — HTML full text](https://arxiv.org/html/2412.19437v1)
- [DeepSeek-R1 Paper — HTML full text](https://arxiv.org/html/2501.12948v1)
- [The DeepSeek Series: A Technical Overview — Martin Fowler](https://martinfowler.com/articles/deepseek-papers.html)
- [GRPO — Group Relative Policy Optimization explainer (Cameron Wolfe)](https://cameronrwolfe.substack.com/p/grpo)
- [DeepSeekMath: GRPO original paper (arXiv:2402.03300)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.03300)
- [DeepSeek-V3.2 Release Notes](https://api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news251201)
- [AI Agents Weekly: GPT-5.5, DeepSeek-V4 Preview, Kimi K2.6 Agent Swarm](https://nlp.elvissaravia.com/p/ai-agents-weekly-gpt-55-deepseek-v4-preview-kimi-k2-6-agent-swarm)
- [China's DeepSeek Preps AI Agent for End-2025 — Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-09-04/deepseek-targets-ai-agent-release-by-end-of-year-to-rival-openai)
- [DeepSeek's Multi-Head Latent Attention — Lior Sinai](https://liorsinai.github.io/machine-learning/2025/02/22/mla.html)
- [Enhancing DeepSeek models with MLA and FP8 in vLLM — Red Hat](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/enhancing-deepseek-models-mla-and-fp8-optimizations-vllm)
- [DeepSeek v3 and R1 Model Architecture — Fireworks AI](https://fireworks.ai/blog/deepseek-model-architecture)
- [Build AI Agents with DeepSeek-R1 NIM — NVIDIA](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/build-ai-agents-with-expert-reasoning-capabilities-using-deepseek-r1-nim/)
- [DeepSeek - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DeepSeek)

content.text:
Now I have comprehensive research across all the requested dimensions. Let me compile the final synthesis.

---

# Multi-Agent Task Decomposition: State of Theory and Practice (2024-2026)

This report synthesizes current academic research and practical framework implementations on how to split complex tasks among multiple concurrent subagents.

---

## Part 1: Academic Survey Landscape

### Key Survey Papers

**"Large Language Model based Multi-Agents: A Survey of Progress and Challenges"**
arXiv:2402.01680 — Published April 2024, presented at IJCAI 2024
- Covers agent profiling, communication, and capacity growth in multi-agent LLM systems
- Identifies three core reasoning frameworks for multi-agent task decomposition: (1) multi-stage serial pipelines, (2) collective decision-making (voting/debate), and (3) self-refine loops
- GitHub companion: https://github.com/taichengguo/LLM_MultiAgents_Survey_Papers

**"A Survey on LLM-based Multi-Agent System: Recent Advances and New Frontiers"**
arXiv:2412.17481 — December 2024 / January 2025
- Taxonomizes applications into task-solving, simulation, and evaluation domains
- Notes that dependency management and multi-level context alignment remain largely unsolved open problems
- Available at: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481

**"LLM Multi-Agent Systems: Challenges and Open Problems"**
arXiv:2402.03578 — Updated May 2025
- Identifies four critical alignment challenges unique to multi-agent decomposition: (1) overall context alignment, (2) inter-agent context alignment, (3) decomposed task alignment, and (4) objective consistency across levels
- Provides a taxonomy of seven single-agent decomposition methods (CoT, ToT, GoT, etc.) as foundations for multi-agent approaches
- Notes that "aligning the complex context with the decomposed tasks during reasoning remains unresolved"
- https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2

**"A Taxonomy of Hierarchical Multi-Agent Systems"**
arXiv:2508.12683 — 2025
- The most rigorous formal taxonomy found. Proposes a **five-axis characterization** of hierarchical multi-agent systems (HMAS):
  1. Control Hierarchy (centralized → decentralized → hybrid)
  2. Information Flow (top-down / bottom-up / lateral)
  3. Role and Task Delegation (fixed vs. emergent roles)
  4. Temporal Hierarchy (strategic long-horizon vs. tactical short-horizon)
  5. Communication Structure (static vs. dynamic topologies)
- Maps six coordination mechanisms: Contract Net Protocol, Auction/Market-based, Consensus algorithms, Feudal/Hierarchical MARL, Organizational structures (GAIA/AGR), and Agent Platforms
- https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683

**"The Orchestration of Multi-Agent Systems: Architectures, Protocols, and Enterprise Adoption"**
arXiv:2601.13671 — January 2026
- Defines a three-layer architecture: Specialized Agents (execution), Orchestration Layer (planning, policy, execution control, state/knowledge, quality/ops), and Communication Protocols (MCP + A2A)
- Task decomposition is driven by a "goal-decomposition engine" that determines tasks and ordering while a "policy unit embeds governance constraints"
- https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1

---

## Part 2: Formal Decomposition Taxonomy

Drawing across all surveyed papers, task decomposition in multi-agent systems maps onto several orthogonal dimensions:

### Dimension 1: Decomposition Strategy Type

| Strategy | Description | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM-driven (zero-shot)** | The orchestrator LLM freely generates the task breakdown | Novel, variable tasks |
| **LLM-driven (few-shot)** | Examples guide the breakdown format | When output structure matters |
| **Programmatic / SOP-based** | Hardcoded decomposition logic for known task classes | Recurring, predictable workflows |
| **HTN (Hierarchical Task Network)** | Abstract goals refined into primitive executable steps | Complex workflows with reusable sub-procedures |
| **DAG-based dynamic** | Tasks represented as nodes with dependency edges; generated at runtime | Mixed parallel/sequential tasks with explicit prerequisites |
| **ACONIC / constraint-based** | Task modeled as satisfiability problem; treewidth measures complexity | Formal reasoning tasks where correctness guarantees matter |

### Dimension 2: Decomposition Granularity

- **Coarse-grained**: Few large subtasks — minimal management overhead, less parallelism opportunity
- **Fine-grained**: Many small subtasks — high parallelism potential, complex dependency tracking required
- **Critical path aware**: Identifies the longest dependency chain and optimizes specifically to shorten it

The research in arXiv:2410.22457 shows that "explicitly mentioning decomposition strategies allowed the system to adapt based on complexity and interdependence" with measurable accuracy improvements.

### Dimension 3: Execution Topology

| Topology | Structure | Parallel? | Control |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Sequential / Pipeline** | Fixed linear stages, each builds on prior | No | Deterministic |
| **Concurrent / Fan-out** | Same input dispatched to multiple agents simultaneously | Yes | Scatter-gather |
| **Hierarchical** | Tree of supervisors and workers | Partial (within levels) | Centralized |
| **Handoff / Routing** | Control transfers from one agent to another dynamically | No (one active at a time) | Dynamic |
| **Swarm** | Decentralized agents sharing state, no central coordinator | Yes | Emergent |
| **Mesh** | Explicit peer-to-peer connections in small teams | Partial | Peer-driven |
| **Magentic / Task-ledger** | Manager builds and refines a task ledger dynamically | Partial | Adaptive |

---

## Part 3: Three Design Principles (AOP Framework)

The Agent-Oriented Planning paper (arXiv:2410.02189, OpenReview, October 2024) provides the clearest formal principles for correct decomposition:

1. **Solvability**: Every subtask must be independently and completely resolvable by at least one available agent in the system. Generating subtasks no agent can handle is a primary source of failure.

2. **Completeness**: The union of all subtasks must cover the full original query. Information loss in decomposition cascades into incomplete final answers.

3. **Non-Redundancy**: Subtasks must not overlap. Without this, agents duplicate work and produce conflicting outputs that are difficult to aggregate.

These three properties map directly onto the empirical failure taxonomy from the "Why Your Multi-Agent System is Failing" analysis, which found unstructured "bag of agents" systems amplify errors by up to **17x** compared to single-agent baselines.

---

## Part 4: Framework Implementations

### LangGraph (LangChain)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Graph-topology-based. You define nodes and edges; LangGraph automatically runs nodes with multiple incoming dependencies concurrently (fan-out = parallel "superstep").
- **Role assignment**: Nodes are declared with specific tools, models, and prompts. A Supervisor node (using `langgraph-supervisor`) dynamically routes to worker subgraphs.
- **Parallel mechanism**: The **Send API** enables runtime-dynamic map-reduce: a routing function returns a list of `Send(node, state)` objects; LangGraph fires all in parallel and waits at convergence.
- **Dependency management**: Reducer functions merge concurrent state writes. Atomic failure — if one parallel branch fails, the entire superstep fails.
- **Result aggregation**: Reducer functions on shared state keys. A `defer=True` flag ensures downstream nodes wait for all parallel branches.
- **Measured speedup**: 137x in one documented case (61s → 0.45s) for a research assistant with independent tool calls.
- **Docs**: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/

### AutoGen (Microsoft)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Four patterns — (1) function-based planner generates 3–5 subtasks, (2) group chat with auto speaker selection, (3) group chat with custom deterministic routing, (4) AutoBuild dynamic agent generation.
- **Role assignment**: Typed agents: `AssistantAgent` (LLM-driven), `UserProxyAgent` (human-in-loop), `ToolAgent` (code/API), `GroupChatManager` (routing coordinator). Agents receive role descriptions plus tool constraints.
- **Dependency management**: Custom speaker selection functions check execution results and conditionally route (e.g., on error, re-route to engineer).
- **Result aggregation**: Structured reflection — summaries compiled from multi-agent conversations.
- **Docs**: https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/topics/task_decomposition/

### CrewAI
- **Decomposition strategy**: Task-as-object model. Each `Task` has: description, expected output, assigned agent, optional context (dependencies), optional tools, async flag.
- **Role assignment**: Role-based. Each agent has `role`, `goal`, `backstory`, and tool access. Role boundaries reduce hallucinations. The manager in `Process.hierarchical` dynamically delegates.
- **Dependency management**: `context` attribute on a task references predecessor task outputs explicitly. Sequential mode auto-passes outputs; async tasks can be gathered by later tasks.
- **Execution modes**: Sequential (linear), Hierarchical (manager delegates), Async (`async_execution=True` for individual tasks).
- **Result aggregation**: `TaskOutput` objects with raw, JSON, or Pydantic formats. The final task in a crew accumulates context from all prior tasks.
- **Docs**: https://docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/tasks

### MetaGPT (arXiv:2308.00352, ICLR 2024 Oral)
- **Decomposition strategy**: SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) encoding. Roles are pre-assigned to mirror a software company: Product Manager → Architect → Project Manager → Engineer → QA Engineer.
- **Role assignment**: Fixed at design time. Each role has `profile`, `goal`, `constraints`, and domain-specific capabilities.
- **Communication**: Agents pass **structured documents** (PRDs, system designs, task lists, code) rather than natural language dialogue. A **publish-subscribe message pool** lets agents subscribe only to relevant messages.
- **Dependency management**: Sequential activation — each role waits for structured documents from upstream roles. Single-source-of-truth shared documentation.
- **Result aggregation**: Assembly-line convergence — executable code emerges only after all five role stages validate.
- **Performance**: 85.9–87.7% Pass@1 on HumanEval, outperforms prior chat-based systems on collaborative SE benchmarks.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352

### OpenAI Swarm / Agents SDK
- **Decomposition strategy**: Routines + Handoffs. A routine is a set of steps and tools; a handoff transfers control to a more appropriate agent.
- **Role assignment**: Each agent = instructions + tools. Triage agents route to specialists via `handoff()` functions (returning an `Agent` object).
- **Parallel execution**: Swarm itself is sequential handoff; the production Agents SDK supports `asyncio.gather` for parallel execution of independent agents.
- **Code-driven alternatives**: Sequential chaining, parallel `asyncio.gather`, iterative refinement loops, structured-output classification routing.
- **Result aggregation**: "Agents as tools" pattern — a manager agent calls specialists via `Agent.as_tool()` and synthesizes the final answer.
- **Docs**: https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/multi_agent/ | https://github.com/openai/swarm

### MegaAgent (arXiv:2408.09955, ACL 2025 Findings)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Fully autonomous, SOP-free recursive decomposition. Boss Agent → Admin Agents → Ordinary Agents, where any admin can recruit sub-agents on demand.
- **Role assignment**: Emergent — admin agents assess task complexity and autonomously determine what roles to recruit. No predefined SOP.
- **Parallel execution**: Agent groups at the same hierarchical level run in parallel, achieving **O(log n)** time complexity vs. O(n) for sequential.
- **Dependency management**: Two-layer coordination: intra-group chat for collaboration within teams, inter-group chat via admin agents for cross-group dependencies. Message queue with producer-consumer paradigm and three states (Idle, Processing, Response).
- **Result aggregation**: Three-level monitoring — agent-level checklists, group-level admin validation, system-level Boss Agent consistency check.
- **Scale**: Demonstrated with up to 590 agents in a national policy simulation.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955

### AgentOrchestra (arXiv:2506.12508, June 2025, v4 Jan 2026)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Planning agent + TEA (Tool-Environment-Agent) Protocol. Planning agent uses a "todo" tool that tracks each subtask with id, description, parameters, priority, category, status, result.
- **Role assignment**: Four specialist subagents: Deep Researcher (web search + information retrieval), Browser Use Agent (DOM/pixel interaction via Playwright), Deep Analyzer (multimodal reasoning across text/code/video/audio), Tool Manager (creates and registers new tools on demand).
- **Dependency management**: Planning agent maintains global execution state; todo list drives sequential delegation. Sub-agents report back, triggering plan updates.
- **Result aggregation**: Planning agent aggregates feedback from all sub-agents, dynamically updates the plan, and calls a "done" tool to signal completion.
- **Performance**: 89.04% on GAIA benchmark (state-of-the-art as of publication).
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508

### TDAG (arXiv:2402.10178, Neural Networks 2025)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Dynamic DAG-based sequential decomposition with adaptive updates. Each subtask is updated after predecessors complete: `t_i' = Update(t_i, r_1, ..., r_{i-1})`.
- **Note**: Despite using a DAG representation, TDAG executes **sequentially** — the DAG captures dependency structure, not parallelism. Each subagent is freshly generated per subtask using tool documentation enhancement and a skill library.
- **Dependency management**: Dynamic replanning after each subtask based on actual results, preventing error propagation from fixed plans.
- **Result aggregation**: Main agent synthesizes all subtask results: `R ← MainAgent.Submit(t_1..t_n, r_1..r_n)`.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178

### DyLAN — Dynamic LLM-Agent Network (arXiv:2310.02170, COLM 2024)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Agent team dynamically selected per query from a candidate pool. Multi-round collaboration modeled as a temporal feed-forward network (T-FFN).
- **Role assignment**: Team Optimization phase selects the most contributory agents unsupervised; an LLM ranker mid-execution deactivates low-performing agents.
- **Dependency management**: Temporal layered communication — each round corresponds to a network layer.
- **Result aggregation**: Final layer aggregates surviving agent outputs.
- **Performance**: Up to 25% accuracy improvement on MMLU via team optimization.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170

---

## Part 5: Practical Heuristics for Task Splitting

### Anthropic's Production Rules (from the multi-agent research system)
Derived from their production system that outperformed single-agent Opus 4 by **90.2%** using Opus 4 as orchestrator + Sonnet 4 as subagents:

| Query Complexity | Agent Count | Tool Calls per Agent |
|---|---|---|
| Simple fact-finding | 1 agent | 3–10 |
| Direct comparisons | 2–4 agents | 10–15 each |
| Complex research | 10+ agents | 15+ each |

Key requirements for each subagent's brief:
1. A concrete, specific objective
2. Explicit task boundary (what NOT to investigate)
3. Required output format
4. Guidance on which tools to use and when to stop

Critical failure mode: Vague decomposition ("research the semiconductor shortage") causes agents to duplicate each other's searches.

### Azure Architecture Center Decision Table (Microsoft, 2026)
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns

| Use Case | Recommended Pattern |
|---|---|
| Clear linear dependencies, no parallelism possible | Sequential |
| Independent analyses of same input, latency-sensitive | Concurrent |
| Consensus-building, iterative validation (brainstorming) | Group Chat |
| Right specialist unknown at start, expertise emerges | Handoff |
| Open-ended, no predetermined solution path | Magentic (task-ledger) |

**Pre-condition for any multi-agent approach**: Start with a single agent + tools. Only add multi-agent coordination when a single agent demonstrably fails due to prompt complexity, tool overload, or security boundary requirements.

### The 5-Pattern Decision Matrix (from orchestration taxonomy research)

| If tasks are... | Use... | Watch for... |
|---|---|---|
| Independent subtasks, no inter-agent communication needed | Orchestrator-Worker | Orchestrator context window bottleneck at 50+ results |
| Fixed sequence with clear stage boundaries | Pipeline | Sequential latency compounds, 5 stages = 10+ sec min |
| 3–8 tight collaborators iterating on shared artifact | Mesh | Combinatorial explosion above ~8 agents |
| Large unknown problem space requiring exploration | Swarm | Poor observability, hard to enforce ordering |
| 20+ agents, multi-domain enterprise scale | Hierarchical | Latency compounds per level (2-3s minimum/level) |

### LangGraph-Specific Parallelization Rules
- **Parallelize when**: Tasks have no shared output dependencies (verifiable by DAG edge analysis)
- **Use Send API when**: The number of parallel tasks is only known at runtime (variable document sets, dynamic query interpretations)
- **Use subgraphs with isolated state when**: Agent encapsulation matters more than seamless communication
- **Note**: Parallel failures are atomic — if one branch fails, all branches in that superstep fail. Design for this.

---

## Part 6: Dependency Management Patterns

Across frameworks, five dependency management strategies appear:

1. **Sequential gating**: Agent B only starts when Agent A's output lands in shared state (LangGraph reducers, CrewAI context attribute, MetaGPT document passing).
2. **Dynamic replanning**: After each subtask, the orchestrator revises remaining tasks based on actual results rather than initial plan (TDAG update function, AgentOrchestra planning loop, MegaAgent admin oversight).
3. **Publish-subscribe**: Agents subscribe to message types relevant to their role and consume only what they need (MetaGPT's shared message pool).
4. **Contract Net Protocol**: Orchestrator broadcasts task; agents bid; winner is assigned (classical HMAS approach identified in the taxonomy paper arXiv:2508.12683).
5. **Blackboard / shared state**: All agents read and write to a common data structure with conflict resolution via reducers (LangGraph state, Swarm's shared context).

---

## Part 7: Result Aggregation Patterns

| Aggregation Method | Best For | Used In |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM synthesis** — orchestrator reads all sub-results and generates narrative summary | Research, analysis, open-ended tasks | Anthropic multi-agent system, AgentOrchestra |
| **Voting / majority rule** | Classification, binary decisions | DyLAN, Group Chat pattern |
| **Weighted merging** | Scored recommendations | Concurrent orchestration with quality weights |
| **Structured relay** — output of each task becomes input of next | Linear pipelines | CrewAI sequential, MetaGPT SOP chain |
| **Pydantic / schema validation** — outputs must conform to type before aggregation | Reliable structured output | CrewAI `output_pydantic`, AutoGen reflection |
| **Hierarchical fusion** — local results summarized at each level before upward transmission | Large-scale hierarchical systems | MegaAgent, hierarchical HMAS |
| **Blackboard consolidation** — agents post to shared store; final agent reads and integrates | Loosely coupled teams | Swarm-style architectures |

---

## Part 8: Known Failure Modes

1. **Bag of Agents**: No structured topology → errors amplify 17x. Each agent's error becomes the next agent's input.
2. **Vague decomposition**: Subtasks not assigned to agents capable of solving them (violates Solvability). Agents default to duplicate searches.
3. **Completeness gaps**: Decomposition loses information from the original query; sub-answers can't reconstruct the whole.
4. **Redundant subtasks**: Multiple agents investigate the same angle, wasting tokens and producing conflicting answers.
5. **Context window bottleneck**: In Orchestrator-Worker with 50+ sub-results, the orchestrator's context window becomes the limiting factor.
6. **Atomic failure propagation**: In LangGraph parallel supersteps, one failing branch kills all parallel work in that step.
7. **Latency stacking in hierarchies**: Each level adds 2–3 seconds minimum; a 4-level hierarchy has a 10-second floor before any work completes.

---

## Key Papers and Links

- arXiv:2402.01680 — LLM Multi-Agent Survey (IJCAI 2024): https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01680
- arXiv:2412.17481 — LLM-MAS Recent Advances (Jan 2025): https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481
- arXiv:2402.03578 — LLM Multi-Agent Challenges: https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2
- arXiv:2508.12683 — HMAS Taxonomy: https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683
- arXiv:2601.13671 — Orchestration Architectures & Protocols: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1
- arXiv:2410.02189 — Agent-Oriented Planning (AOP): https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2
- arXiv:2308.00352 — MetaGPT (ICLR 2024 Oral): https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
- arXiv:2408.09955 — MegaAgent (ACL 2025): https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955
- arXiv:2506.12508 — AgentOrchestra (Jan 2026): https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508
- arXiv:2402.10178 — TDAG Dynamic Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178
- arXiv:2310.02170 — DyLAN Dynamic Agent Network (COLM 2024): https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170
- arXiv:2410.22457 — Advancing Agentic Systems with DAG Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/html/2410.22457v1
- arXiv:2510.07772 — ACONIC Constraint-based Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.07772v1
- Anthropic Engineering Blog: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system
- Azure AI Agent Design Patterns: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns
- AutoGen Task Decomposition Docs: https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/topics/task_decomposition/

- LangGraph Hierarchical Teams: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/
- OpenAI Agents SDK Multi-Agent: https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/multi_agent/

Research the current state of theory and practice around multi-agent task decomposition — specifically how to split complex tasks among multiple subagents working in parallel. The user wants to understand how to divide work effectively when spawning multiple concurrent subagents.

Search for:
1. Academic papers on multi-agent task decomposition (arxiv, Google Scholar)
   - Terms: "multi-agent task decomposition", "LLM agent orchestration", "task planning multi-agent", "hierarchical multi-agent systems"
2. Frameworks and practical implementations:
   - LangGraph multi-agent patterns
   - AutoGen task decomposition
   - CrewAI role-based decomposition
   - OpenAI Swarm / swarm agent patterns
   - MetaGPT role decomposition
3. Specific decomposition strategies/taxonomies:
   - How do researchers categorize task splitting? (functional decomposition, data decomposition, pipeline decomposition, etc.)
   - What heuristics exist for deciding what goes to which agent?
   - How are dependencies between subtasks managed?
4. Look for survey papers on LLM multi-agent systems from 2024-2025

For each approach/paper found, note:
- The decomposition strategy used
- How agents are assigned roles
- How results are aggregated
- Any formal taxonomy or classification scheme

Include arxiv links and framework documentation URLs. Focus on practical guidance, not just theoretical abstractions.

content.text:
Now I have comprehensive research across all the requested dimensions. Let me compile the final synthesis.

---

# Multi-Agent Task Decomposition: State of Theory and Practice (2024-2026)

This report synthesizes current academic research and practical framework implementations on how to split complex tasks among multiple concurrent subagents.

---

## Part 1: Academic Survey Landscape

### Key Survey Papers

**"Large Language Model based Multi-Agents: A Survey of Progress and Challenges"**
arXiv:2402.01680 — Published April 2024, presented at IJCAI 2024
- Covers agent profiling, communication, and capacity growth in multi-agent LLM systems
- Identifies three core reasoning frameworks for multi-agent task decomposition: (1) multi-stage serial pipelines, (2) collective decision-making (voting/debate), and (3) self-refine loops
- GitHub companion: https://github.com/taichengguo/LLM_MultiAgents_Survey_Papers

**"A Survey on LLM-based Multi-Agent System: Recent Advances and New Frontiers"**
arXiv:2412.17481 — December 2024 / January 2025
- Taxonomizes applications into task-solving, simulation, and evaluation domains
- Notes that dependency management and multi-level context alignment remain largely unsolved open problems
- Available at: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481

**"LLM Multi-Agent Systems: Challenges and Open Problems"**
arXiv:2402.03578 — Updated May 2025
- Identifies four critical alignment challenges unique to multi-agent decomposition: (1) overall context alignment, (2) inter-agent context alignment, (3) decomposed task alignment, and (4) objective consistency across levels
- Provides a taxonomy of seven single-agent decomposition methods (CoT, ToT, GoT, etc.) as foundations for multi-agent approaches
- Notes that "aligning the complex context with the decomposed tasks during reasoning remains unresolved"
- https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2

**"A Taxonomy of Hierarchical Multi-Agent Systems"**
arXiv:2508.12683 — 2025
- The most rigorous formal taxonomy found. Proposes a **five-axis characterization** of hierarchical multi-agent systems (HMAS):
  1. Control Hierarchy (centralized → decentralized → hybrid)
  2. Information Flow (top-down / bottom-up / lateral)
  3. Role and Task Delegation (fixed vs. emergent roles)
  4. Temporal Hierarchy (strategic long-horizon vs. tactical short-horizon)
  5. Communication Structure (static vs. dynamic topologies)
- Maps six coordination mechanisms: Contract Net Protocol, Auction/Market-based, Consensus algorithms, Feudal/Hierarchical MARL, Organizational structures (GAIA/AGR), and Agent Platforms
- https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683

**"The Orchestration of Multi-Agent Systems: Architectures, Protocols, and Enterprise Adoption"**
arXiv:2601.13671 — January 2026
- Defines a three-layer architecture: Specialized Agents (execution), Orchestration Layer (planning, policy, execution control, state/knowledge, quality/ops), and Communication Protocols (MCP + A2A)
- Task decomposition is driven by a "goal-decomposition engine" that determines tasks and ordering while a "policy unit embeds governance constraints"
- https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1

---

## Part 2: Formal Decomposition Taxonomy

Drawing across all surveyed papers, task decomposition in multi-agent systems maps onto several orthogonal dimensions:

### Dimension 1: Decomposition Strategy Type

| Strategy | Description | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM-driven (zero-shot)** | The orchestrator LLM freely generates the task breakdown | Novel, variable tasks |
| **LLM-driven (few-shot)** | Examples guide the breakdown format | When output structure matters |
| **Programmatic / SOP-based** | Hardcoded decomposition logic for known task classes | Recurring, predictable workflows |
| **HTN (Hierarchical Task Network)** | Abstract goals refined into primitive executable steps | Complex workflows with reusable sub-procedures |
| **DAG-based dynamic** | Tasks represented as nodes with dependency edges; generated at runtime | Mixed parallel/sequential tasks with explicit prerequisites |
| **ACONIC / constraint-based** | Task modeled as satisfiability problem; treewidth measures complexity | Formal reasoning tasks where correctness guarantees matter |

### Dimension 2: Decomposition Granularity

- **Coarse-grained**: Few large subtasks — minimal management overhead, less parallelism opportunity
- **Fine-grained**: Many small subtasks — high parallelism potential, complex dependency tracking required
- **Critical path aware**: Identifies the longest dependency chain and optimizes specifically to shorten it

The research in arXiv:2410.22457 shows that "explicitly mentioning decomposition strategies allowed the system to adapt based on complexity and interdependence" with measurable accuracy improvements.

### Dimension 3: Execution Topology

| Topology | Structure | Parallel? | Control |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Sequential / Pipeline** | Fixed linear stages, each builds on prior | No | Deterministic |
| **Concurrent / Fan-out** | Same input dispatched to multiple agents simultaneously | Yes | Scatter-gather |
| **Hierarchical** | Tree of supervisors and workers | Partial (within levels) | Centralized |
| **Handoff / Routing** | Control transfers from one agent to another dynamically | No (one active at a time) | Dynamic |
| **Swarm** | Decentralized agents sharing state, no central coordinator | Yes | Emergent |
| **Mesh** | Explicit peer-to-peer connections in small teams | Partial | Peer-driven |
| **Magentic / Task-ledger** | Manager builds and refines a task ledger dynamically | Partial | Adaptive |

---

## Part 3: Three Design Principles (AOP Framework)

The Agent-Oriented Planning paper (arXiv:2410.02189, OpenReview, October 2024) provides the clearest formal principles for correct decomposition:

1. **Solvability**: Every subtask must be independently and completely resolvable by at least one available agent in the system. Generating subtasks no agent can handle is a primary source of failure.

2. **Completeness**: The union of all subtasks must cover the full original query. Information loss in decomposition cascades into incomplete final answers.

3. **Non-Redundancy**: Subtasks must not overlap. Without this, agents duplicate work and produce conflicting outputs that are difficult to aggregate.

These three properties map directly onto the empirical failure taxonomy from the "Why Your Multi-Agent System is Failing" analysis, which found unstructured "bag of agents" systems amplify errors by up to **17x** compared to single-agent baselines.

---

## Part 4: Framework Implementations

### LangGraph (LangChain)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Graph-topology-based. You define nodes and edges; LangGraph automatically runs nodes with multiple incoming dependencies concurrently (fan-out = parallel "superstep").
- **Role assignment**: Nodes are declared with specific tools, models, and prompts. A Supervisor node (using `langgraph-supervisor`) dynamically routes to worker subgraphs.
- **Parallel mechanism**: The **Send API** enables runtime-dynamic map-reduce: a routing function returns a list of `Send(node, state)` objects; LangGraph fires all in parallel and waits at convergence.
- **Dependency management**: Reducer functions merge concurrent state writes. Atomic failure — if one parallel branch fails, the entire superstep fails.
- **Result aggregation**: Reducer functions on shared state keys. A `defer=True` flag ensures downstream nodes wait for all parallel branches.
- **Measured speedup**: 137x in one documented case (61s → 0.45s) for a research assistant with independent tool calls.
- **Docs**: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/

### AutoGen (Microsoft)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Four patterns — (1) function-based planner generates 3–5 subtasks, (2) group chat with auto speaker selection, (3) group chat with custom deterministic routing, (4) AutoBuild dynamic agent generation.
- **Role assignment**: Typed agents: `AssistantAgent` (LLM-driven), `UserProxyAgent` (human-in-loop), `ToolAgent` (code/API), `GroupChatManager` (routing coordinator). Agents receive role descriptions plus tool constraints.
- **Dependency management**: Custom speaker selection functions check execution results and conditionally route (e.g., on error, re-route to engineer).
- **Result aggregation**: Structured reflection — summaries compiled from multi-agent conversations.
- **Docs**: https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/topics/task_decomposition/

### CrewAI
- **Decomposition strategy**: Task-as-object model. Each `Task` has: description, expected output, assigned agent, optional context (dependencies), optional tools, async flag.
- **Role assignment**: Role-based. Each agent has `role`, `goal`, `backstory`, and tool access. Role boundaries reduce hallucinations. The manager in `Process.hierarchical` dynamically delegates.
- **Dependency management**: `context` attribute on a task references predecessor task outputs explicitly. Sequential mode auto-passes outputs; async tasks can be gathered by later tasks.
- **Execution modes**: Sequential (linear), Hierarchical (manager delegates), Async (`async_execution=True` for individual tasks).
- **Result aggregation**: `TaskOutput` objects with raw, JSON, or Pydantic formats. The final task in a crew accumulates context from all prior tasks.
- **Docs**: https://docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/tasks

### MetaGPT (arXiv:2308.00352, ICLR 2024 Oral)
- **Decomposition strategy**: SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) encoding. Roles are pre-assigned to mirror a software company: Product Manager → Architect → Project Manager → Engineer → QA Engineer.
- **Role assignment**: Fixed at design time. Each role has `profile`, `goal`, `constraints`, and domain-specific capabilities.
- **Communication**: Agents pass **structured documents** (PRDs, system designs, task lists, code) rather than natural language dialogue. A **publish-subscribe message pool** lets agents subscribe only to relevant messages.
- **Dependency management**: Sequential activation — each role waits for structured documents from upstream roles. Single-source-of-truth shared documentation.
- **Result aggregation**: Assembly-line convergence — executable code emerges only after all five role stages validate.
- **Performance**: 85.9–87.7% Pass@1 on HumanEval, outperforms prior chat-based systems on collaborative SE benchmarks.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352

### OpenAI Swarm / Agents SDK
- **Decomposition strategy**: Routines + Handoffs. A routine is a set of steps and tools; a handoff transfers control to a more appropriate agent.
- **Role assignment**: Each agent = instructions + tools. Triage agents route to specialists via `handoff()` functions (returning an `Agent` object).
- **Parallel execution**: Swarm itself is sequential handoff; the production Agents SDK supports `asyncio.gather` for parallel execution of independent agents.
- **Code-driven alternatives**: Sequential chaining, parallel `asyncio.gather`, iterative refinement loops, structured-output classification routing.
- **Result aggregation**: "Agents as tools" pattern — a manager agent calls specialists via `Agent.as_tool()` and synthesizes the final answer.
- **Docs**: https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/multi_agent/ | https://github.com/openai/swarm

### MegaAgent (arXiv:2408.09955, ACL 2025 Findings)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Fully autonomous, SOP-free recursive decomposition. Boss Agent → Admin Agents → Ordinary Agents, where any admin can recruit sub-agents on demand.
- **Role assignment**: Emergent — admin agents assess task complexity and autonomously determine what roles to recruit. No predefined SOP.
- **Parallel execution**: Agent groups at the same hierarchical level run in parallel, achieving **O(log n)** time complexity vs. O(n) for sequential.
- **Dependency management**: Two-layer coordination: intra-group chat for collaboration within teams, inter-group chat via admin agents for cross-group dependencies. Message queue with producer-consumer paradigm and three states (Idle, Processing, Response).
- **Result aggregation**: Three-level monitoring — agent-level checklists, group-level admin validation, system-level Boss Agent consistency check.
- **Scale**: Demonstrated with up to 590 agents in a national policy simulation.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955

### AgentOrchestra (arXiv:2506.12508, June 2025, v4 Jan 2026)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Planning agent + TEA (Tool-Environment-Agent) Protocol. Planning agent uses a "todo" tool that tracks each subtask with id, description, parameters, priority, category, status, result.
- **Role assignment**: Four specialist subagents: Deep Researcher (web search + information retrieval), Browser Use Agent (DOM/pixel interaction via Playwright), Deep Analyzer (multimodal reasoning across text/code/video/audio), Tool Manager (creates and registers new tools on demand).
- **Dependency management**: Planning agent maintains global execution state; todo list drives sequential delegation. Sub-agents report back, triggering plan updates.
- **Result aggregation**: Planning agent aggregates feedback from all sub-agents, dynamically updates the plan, and calls a "done" tool to signal completion.
- **Performance**: 89.04% on GAIA benchmark (state-of-the-art as of publication).
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508

### TDAG (arXiv:2402.10178, Neural Networks 2025)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Dynamic DAG-based sequential decomposition with adaptive updates. Each subtask is updated after predecessors complete: `t_i' = Update(t_i, r_1, ..., r_{i-1})`.
- **Note**: Despite using a DAG representation, TDAG executes **sequentially** — the DAG captures dependency structure, not parallelism. Each subagent is freshly generated per subtask using tool documentation enhancement and a skill library.
- **Dependency management**: Dynamic replanning after each subtask based on actual results, preventing error propagation from fixed plans.
- **Result aggregation**: Main agent synthesizes all subtask results: `R ← MainAgent.Submit(t_1..t_n, r_1..r_n)`.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178

### DyLAN — Dynamic LLM-Agent Network (arXiv:2310.02170, COLM 2024)
- **Decomposition strategy**: Agent team dynamically selected per query from a candidate pool. Multi-round collaboration modeled as a temporal feed-forward network (T-FFN).
- **Role assignment**: Team Optimization phase selects the most contributory agents unsupervised; an LLM ranker mid-execution deactivates low-performing agents.
- **Dependency management**: Temporal layered communication — each round corresponds to a network layer.
- **Result aggregation**: Final layer aggregates surviving agent outputs.
- **Performance**: Up to 25% accuracy improvement on MMLU via team optimization.
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170

---

## Part 5: Practical Heuristics for Task Splitting

### Anthropic's Production Rules (from the multi-agent research system)
Derived from their production system that outperformed single-agent Opus 4 by **90.2%** using Opus 4 as orchestrator + Sonnet 4 as subagents:

| Query Complexity | Agent Count | Tool Calls per Agent |
|---|---|---|
| Simple fact-finding | 1 agent | 3–10 |
| Direct comparisons | 2–4 agents | 10–15 each |
| Complex research | 10+ agents | 15+ each |

Key requirements for each subagent's brief:
1. A concrete, specific objective
2. Explicit task boundary (what NOT to investigate)
3. Required output format
4. Guidance on which tools to use and when to stop

Critical failure mode: Vague decomposition ("research the semiconductor shortage") causes agents to duplicate each other's searches.

### Azure Architecture Center Decision Table (Microsoft, 2026)
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns

| Use Case | Recommended Pattern |
|---|---|
| Clear linear dependencies, no parallelism possible | Sequential |
| Independent analyses of same input, latency-sensitive | Concurrent |
| Consensus-building, iterative validation (brainstorming) | Group Chat |
| Right specialist unknown at start, expertise emerges | Handoff |
| Open-ended, no predetermined solution path | Magentic (task-ledger) |

**Pre-condition for any multi-agent approach**: Start with a single agent + tools. Only add multi-agent coordination when a single agent demonstrably fails due to prompt complexity, tool overload, or security boundary requirements.

### The 5-Pattern Decision Matrix (from orchestration taxonomy research)

| If tasks are... | Use... | Watch for... |
|---|---|---|
| Independent subtasks, no inter-agent communication needed | Orchestrator-Worker | Orchestrator context window bottleneck at 50+ results |
| Fixed sequence with clear stage boundaries | Pipeline | Sequential latency compounds, 5 stages = 10+ sec min |
| 3–8 tight collaborators iterating on shared artifact | Mesh | Combinatorial explosion above ~8 agents |
| Large unknown problem space requiring exploration | Swarm | Poor observability, hard to enforce ordering |
| 20+ agents, multi-domain enterprise scale | Hierarchical | Latency compounds per level (2-3s minimum/level) |

### LangGraph-Specific Parallelization Rules
- **Parallelize when**: Tasks have no shared output dependencies (verifiable by DAG edge analysis)
- **Use Send API when**: The number of parallel tasks is only known at runtime (variable document sets, dynamic query interpretations)
- **Use subgraphs with isolated state when**: Agent encapsulation matters more than seamless communication
- **Note**: Parallel failures are atomic — if one branch fails, all branches in that superstep fail. Design for this.

---

## Part 6: Dependency Management Patterns

Across frameworks, five dependency management strategies appear:

1. **Sequential gating**: Agent B only starts when Agent A's output lands in shared state (LangGraph reducers, CrewAI context attribute, MetaGPT document passing).
2. **Dynamic replanning**: After each subtask, the orchestrator revises remaining tasks based on actual results rather than initial plan (TDAG update function, AgentOrchestra planning loop, MegaAgent admin oversight).
3. **Publish-subscribe**: Agents subscribe to message types relevant to their role and consume only what they need (MetaGPT's shared message pool).
4. **Contract Net Protocol**: Orchestrator broadcasts task; agents bid; winner is assigned (classical HMAS approach identified in the taxonomy paper arXiv:2508.12683).
5. **Blackboard / shared state**: All agents read and write to a common data structure with conflict resolution via reducers (LangGraph state, Swarm's shared context).

---

## Part 7: Result Aggregation Patterns

| Aggregation Method | Best For | Used In |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM synthesis** — orchestrator reads all sub-results and generates narrative summary | Research, analysis, open-ended tasks | Anthropic multi-agent system, AgentOrchestra |
| **Voting / majority rule** | Classification, binary decisions | DyLAN, Group Chat pattern |
| **Weighted merging** | Scored recommendations | Concurrent orchestration with quality weights |
| **Structured relay** — output of each task becomes input of next | Linear pipelines | CrewAI sequential, MetaGPT SOP chain |
| **Pydantic / schema validation** — outputs must conform to type before aggregation | Reliable structured output | CrewAI `output_pydantic`, AutoGen reflection |
| **Hierarchical fusion** — local results summarized at each level before upward transmission | Large-scale hierarchical systems | MegaAgent, hierarchical HMAS |
| **Blackboard consolidation** — agents post to shared store; final agent reads and integrates | Loosely coupled teams | Swarm-style architectures |

---

## Part 8: Known Failure Modes

1. **Bag of Agents**: No structured topology → errors amplify 17x. Each agent's error becomes the next agent's input.
2. **Vague decomposition**: Subtasks not assigned to agents capable of solving them (violates Solvability). Agents default to duplicate searches.
3. **Completeness gaps**: Decomposition loses information from the original query; sub-answers can't reconstruct the whole.
4. **Redundant subtasks**: Multiple agents investigate the same angle, wasting tokens and producing conflicting answers.
5. **Context window bottleneck**: In Orchestrator-Worker with 50+ sub-results, the orchestrator's context window becomes the limiting factor.
6. **Atomic failure propagation**: In LangGraph parallel supersteps, one failing branch kills all parallel work in that step.
7. **Latency stacking in hierarchies**: Each level adds 2–3 seconds minimum; a 4-level hierarchy has a 10-second floor before any work completes.

---

## Key Papers and Links

- arXiv:2402.01680 — LLM Multi-Agent Survey (IJCAI 2024): https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01680
- arXiv:2412.17481 — LLM-MAS Recent Advances (Jan 2025): https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481
- arXiv:2402.03578 — LLM Multi-Agent Challenges: https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2
- arXiv:2508.12683 — HMAS Taxonomy: https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683
- arXiv:2601.13671 — Orchestration Architectures & Protocols: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1
- arXiv:2410.02189 — Agent-Oriented Planning (AOP): https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2
- arXiv:2308.00352 — MetaGPT (ICLR 2024 Oral): https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
- arXiv:2408.09955 — MegaAgent (ACL 2025): https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955
- arXiv:2506.12508 — AgentOrchestra (Jan 2026): https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508
- arXiv:2402.10178 — TDAG Dynamic Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178
- arXiv:2310.02170 — DyLAN Dynamic Agent Network (COLM 2024): https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170
- arXiv:2410.22457 — Advancing Agentic Systems with DAG Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/html/2410.22457v1
- arXiv:2510.07772 — ACONIC Constraint-based Decomposition: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.07772v1
- Anthropic Engineering Blog: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system
- Azure AI Agent Design Patterns: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns
- AutoGen Task Decomposition Docs: https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/topics/task_decomposition/
- CrewAI Tasks Docs: https://docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/tasks
- LangGraph Hierarchical Teams: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/
- OpenAI Agents SDK Multi-Agent: https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/multi_agent/
- OpenAI Swarm GitHub: https://github.com/openai/swarm
- LangGraph Parallelization Deep-Dive: https://aipractitioner.substack.com/p/scaling-langgraph-agents-parallelization
- Orchestration Patterns (Swarm/Mesh/Hierarchical/Pipeline): https://dev.to/jose_gurusup_dev/agent-orchestration-patterns-swarm-vs-mesh-vs-hierarchical-vs-pipeline-b40

input.content:
# DeepSeek 近期進展 與 多Agent任務分解理論與實務

> 研究日期：2026-04-29

---

## 第一部分：DeepSeek 的真實改進機制

### 結論先說

**DeepSeek V3 / R1 的性能提升與「多Agent協作」無關。** 經查閱 DeepSeek 官方技術報告（arXiv:2412.19437、arXiv:2501.12948），未找到任何關於 agent swarm、多Agent訓練或多Agent推理的機制。「Agent群」的說法可能源自術語混淆。

### 實際改進機制（四大核心）

#### 1. 混合專家架構（MoE）— DeepSeek-V3
- 671B 總參數，每個 token 只激活 37B
- **auxiliary-loss-free 負載均衡**：相較過去 MoE 的新創，不再需要輔助損失函數
- **多 Token 預測（Multi-Token Prediction）**：同時預測多個未來 token，強化表示學習

> 注意：MoE 的「expert」聽起來像 agent，實際上是**單一模型內的子網路路由層**，非多Agent協作。

#### 2. 多頭潛在注意力（MLA）— DeepSeek-V2 & V3
- 將 KV cache 壓縮進低秩潛在空間
- 有效批次容量提升約 **9.6 倍**（相比標準多頭注意力）
- 純矩陣壓縮技術，與 agent 無關

#### 3. 硬體-演算法協同設計 — DeepSeek-V3
- **FP8 混合精度訓練**：首次在兆參數規模驗證，FLOPS 比 FP16 多一倍
- **DualPipe 演算法**：新型管線並行排程，重疊計算與通訊，減少空泡（pipeline bubble）
- **PTX 級 GPU 優化**：warp 層級專化，最大化 H800 利用率
- 全訓練成本：14.8T tokens，僅用 **278.8 萬 H800 GPU 小時**，且無 loss spike

#### 4. 群組相對策略優化（GRPO）— DeepSeek-R1
- 每個 prompt 生成**一組候選輸出**（group of outputs），使用當前 policy 採樣
- 以規則型 reward（正確性 + 格式）評分
- 以**組內平均作為 baseline** 計算相對優勢，取代 PPO 中獨立的 critic/value 網路
- 結果：單一模型通過純 RL 自發產生自我反思、回溯（「aha moments」）、動態推理長度

> 「GRPO 的 group」= 同一模型的一批採樣輸出，**不是多個獨立 agent**

### 術語混淆來源

| 混淆點 | 實際是什麼 |
|---|---|
| MoE 的「experts」 | 單一模型內的子網路，token 路由選擇 |
| GRPO 的「group」 | 同一模型一次採樣的多個候選答案 |
| DeepSeek 做「agent產品」 | Bloomberg 2025/09 報導 DeepSeek 正在**開發面向用戶的 agent 產品**（非訓練機制） |
| Kimi K2.6「Agent Swarm」 | 競爭對手 Kimi 的功能，不是 DeepSeek |
| 第三方整合 | CrewAI、MetaGPT 等**使用** DeepSeek 模型作為組件，不是 DeepSeek 自身架構 |

### 參考文獻
- [DeepSeek-R1 論文 (arXiv:2501.12948)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12948)
- [DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report (arXiv:2412.19437)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19437)
- [Martin Fowler DeepSeek 技術綜覽](https://martinfowler.com/articles/deepseek-papers.html)

---

## 第二部分：多 Agent 任務分解的理論與實務

### 背景：為什麼需要任務分解？

當單一 agent 面臨以下情況時，多 agent 系統才有必要：
- prompt 長度或複雜度超過 context window
- 任務需要不同專業工具組合
- 需要平行加速（獨立子任務可同時進行）
- 安全邊界要求（不同 agent 有不同權限）

**不要過早引入多 agent**：先用單一 agent + 工具，當單 agent 明顯失敗時再分解。

---

### 任務分解的三大正確性原則（AOP Framework）

來源：[Agent-Oriented Planning (arXiv:2410.02189)](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2)

1. **可解性（Solvability）**：每個子任務必須有至少一個 agent 能獨立完整解決。生成無法被任何 agent 解決的子任務是最主要的失敗原因。

2. **完整性（Completeness）**：所有子任務的聯集必須覆蓋原始問題。分解過程中的資訊遺失會使最終答案不完整。

3. **非冗餘性（Non-Redundancy）**：子任務不可重疊。重疊導致 agent 重複勞動，產生衝突輸出難以聚合。

> 違反以上三原則的「agent 大雜燴」系統，錯誤放大可達 **17 倍**。  
> 來源：[Why Your Multi-Agent System is Failing](https://towardsdatascience.com/why-your-multi-agent-system-is-failing-escaping-the-17x-error-trap-of-the-bag-of-agents/)

---

### 分解策略類型

| 策略 | 說明 | 適用時機 |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM 驅動（zero-shot）** | Orchestrator LLM 自由生成任務拆分 | 新穎、多變的任務 |
| **LLM 驅動（few-shot）** | 示例引導拆分格式 | 輸出結構有要求時 |
| **程式化 / SOP** | 固定邏輯拆分已知任務類型 | 重複、可預測的流程 |
| **HTN（層次任務網路）** | 抽象目標逐步精化為可執行步驟 | 含可重用子程序的複雜流程 |
| **DAG 動態生成** | 任務建模為帶依賴邊的節點圖，執行時生成 | 有明確前置條件的混合串/並行任務 |
| **限制式分解（ACONIC）** | 將任務建模為可滿足性問題；樹寬衡量複雜度 | 需要正確性保證的形式推理任務 |

---

### 執行拓撲類型

| 拓撲 | 結構 | 是否並行 | 控制方式 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **順序 / Pipeline** | 固定線性階段，每階段基於前一階段 | 否 | 確定性 |
| **並發 / Fan-out** | 同一輸入分發給多個 agent 同時執行 | 是 | Scatter-Gather |
| **層次（Hierarchical）** | 督導者與工作者組成的樹狀結構 | 層內部分並行 | 集中式 |
| **Handoff / 路由** | 控制動態轉交給更合適的 agent | 否（單一活躍） | 動態 |
| **Swarm** | 去中心化，共享狀態，無中央協調者 | 是 | 湧現式 |
| **Mesh** | 小型團隊內的明確 Peer-to-Peer 連接 | 部分 | 點對點 |
| **Magentic / 任務台帳** | Manager 動態建立與修正任務列表 | 部分 | 自適應 |

---

### 拓撲選擇決策表（Microsoft Azure，2026）

| 使用情況 | 推薦模式 |
|---|---|
| 明確線性依賴，無並行可能 | Sequential |
| 同一輸入的獨立分析，對延遲敏感 | Concurrent |
| 共識構建、迭代驗證（腦力激盪） | Group Chat |
| 開始時不知道需要哪個專家 | Handoff |
| 開放式、無預先解決路徑 | Magentic（任務台帳） |

來源：[Azure AI Agent Design Patterns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns)

---

### Anthropic 生產系統的實際數據

來源：[Anthropic 多 Agent 研究系統部落格](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system)

使用 Opus 4 作為 Orchestrator + Sonnet 4 作為 subagent，性能超越單一 Opus 4 達 **90.2%**。

| 查詢複雜度 | Agent 數量 | 每個 Agent 的工具呼叫數 |
|---|---|---|
| 簡單事實查詢 | 1 個 | 3–10 |
| 直接比較分析 | 2–4 個 | 每個 10–15 |
| 複雜研究任務 | 10+ 個 | 每個 15+ |

**每個 subagent 的任務說明必須包含**：
1. 具體、明確的目標
2. 明確的任務邊界（**不**調查什麼）
3. 要求的輸出格式
4. 使用哪些工具及何時停止的指引

**最主要的失敗模式**：模糊的分解（如「研究半導體短缺」）導致 agent 重複搜尋相同內容。

---

### 主要框架的分解實作方式

#### LangGraph（LangChain）
- **分解策略**：圖拓撲。多個傳入依賴的節點自動並行（fan-out = 並行 superstep）
- **關鍵 API**：**Send API** — 執行時動態 map-reduce，返回 `Send(node, state)` 列表，LangGraph 全部並行執行
- **聚合**：Reducer 函數合併並行狀態寫入
- **注意**：並行失敗是原子性的——一個分支失敗，整個 superstep 的所有分支都失敗
- **實測加速**：137x（61s → 0.45s）
- 文件：https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/

#### AutoGen（Microsoft）
- **分解策略四種模式**：(1) 函數式規劃器生成 3-5 子任務，(2) group chat 自動發言選擇，(3) group chat 自定確定性路由，(4) AutoBuild 動態生成 agent
- **角色類型**：`AssistantAgent`、`UserProxyAgent`、`ToolAgent`、`GroupChatManager`
- 文件：https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/topics/task_decomposition/

#### CrewAI
- **分解策略**：Task 物件模型。每個 `Task` 有：描述、預期輸出、指派 agent、依賴 context、工具、async 旗標
- **執行模式**：Sequential（線性）、Hierarchical（manager 委派）、Async（`async_execution=True`）
- **依賴管理**：`context` 屬性明確引用前置 task 的輸出
- **聚合**：`TaskOutput` 物件，支援 raw / JSON / Pydantic 格式
- 文件：https://docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/tasks

#### MetaGPT
- **分解策略**：SOP 編碼，模擬軟體公司角色：PM → Architect → Project Manager → Engineer → QA
- **通訊**：傳遞**結構化文件**（PRD、系統設計、任務列表、程式碼），而非自然語言對話
- **發佈訂閱**：共享訊息池，agent 只消費相關訊息類型
- **性能**：Pass@1 85.9–87.7%（HumanEval）
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352

#### MegaAgent（ACL 2025）
- **分解策略**：全自主、無 SOP 的遞迴分解。Boss → Admin → Ordinary，admin 按需招募子 agent
- **時間複雜度**：O(log n) vs. 順序的 O(n)
- **依賴管理**：組內 chat（協作）+ 組間 chat via admin（跨組依賴）
- **規模**：最多 590 個 agent 的國家政策模擬
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955

#### AgentOrchestra（arXiv:2506.12508，GAIA SOTA 89.04%）
- **分解策略**：Planning agent + TEA 協議。todo 工具追蹤每個子任務（id、描述、參數、優先級、狀態、結果）
- **四種專業 subagent**：Deep Researcher（網路搜尋）、Browser Use Agent（網頁互動）、Deep Analyzer（多模態推理）、Tool Manager（動態建立新工具）
- **聚合**：Planning agent 匯總回饋，動態更新計劃，呼叫 done 工具標示完成
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508

---

### 依賴管理的五種模式

| 模式 | 說明 | 使用框架 |
|---|---|---|
| **順序閘控** | B 只在 A 的輸出落入共享狀態後才啟動 | LangGraph reducers, CrewAI context |
| **動態重規劃** | 每個子任務完成後根據實際結果修正剩餘任務 | TDAG, AgentOrchestra, MegaAgent |
| **發佈-訂閱** | Agent 訂閱相關訊息類型，只消費所需 | MetaGPT 共享訊息池 |
| **Contract Net** | Orchestrator 廣播任務，agent 競標，贏家被指派 | 古典 HMAS 方法 |
| **黑板 / 共享狀態** | 所有 agent 讀寫同一資料結構，Reducer 解衝突 | LangGraph state, Swarm context |

---

### 結果聚合的七種模式

| 聚合方法 | 最適用 | 使用在 |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM 合成** | 研究、分析、開放式任務 | Anthropic 系統, AgentOrchestra |
| **投票 / 多數決** | 分類、二元決策 | DyLAN, Group Chat |
| **加權合併** | 帶分數的推薦 | 含品質權重的並發協調 |
| **結構化接力** | 線性管線，前一輸出為下一輸入 | CrewAI sequential, MetaGPT |
| **Pydantic 驗證** | 可靠的結構化輸出 | CrewAI `output_pydantic`, AutoGen reflection |
| **層次融合** | 大規模層次系統 | MegaAgent, HMAS |
| **黑板整合** | 鬆耦合團隊 | Swarm 架構 |

---

### 主要失敗模式

1. **Agent 大雜燴（Bag of Agents）**：無結構拓撲 → 錯誤放大 17 倍
2. **模糊分解**：子任務沒有 agent 能解決（違反可解性）
3. **完整性缺口**：分解丟失原始問題資訊
4. **冗餘子任務**：多個 agent 調查同一角度，產生衝突答案
5. **Context Window 瓶頸**：Orchestrator-Worker 超過 50 個子結果時，orchestrator 成為限制
6. **原子性失敗傳播**：LangGraph 並行 superstep 一個分支失敗，全部失敗
7. **層次延遲堆疊**：每層至少 2-3 秒，4 層層次系統有 10 秒基礎延遲

---

### 五種模式的決策矩陣（拓撲選擇）

| 任務特性 | 使用模式 | 注意事項 |
|---|---|---|
| 獨立子任務，無需跨 agent 通訊 | Orchestrator-Worker | 超過 50 個結果時 orchestrator context 瓶頸 |
| 有清晰階段邊界的固定序列 | Pipeline | 5 個階段最少 10+ 秒 |
| 3–8 個緊密協作者迭代共享成果 | Mesh | 超過 8 個 agent 組合爆炸 |
| 大型未知問題空間需要探索 | Swarm | 可觀察性差，難以強制排序 |
| 20+ agent 的多領域企業規模 | Hierarchical | 每層最少 2-3 秒延遲 |

---

## 學術調查論文（2024-2026 精選）

| 論文 | 核心貢獻 | 連結 |
|---|---|---|
| LLM Multi-Agent Survey (IJCAI 2024) | 三種推理框架分類：多階段串行、集體決策、自我精煉 | [arXiv:2402.01680](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01680) |
| LLM-MAS 近期進展 (2025/01) | 分類應用：任務解決、模擬、評估；指出依賴管理仍未解決 | [arXiv:2412.17481](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481) |
| LLM Multi-Agent 挑戰 (2025/05) | 四大對齊挑戰；7 種單 agent 分解方法分類 | [arXiv:2402.03578](https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2) |
| HMAS 分類學 (2025) | 五軸特徵化 + 六種協調機制 | [arXiv:2508.12683](https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683) |
| 多 Agent 協調架構與協議 (2026/01) | 三層架構：專業 agent + 協調層 + 通訊協議（MCP+A2A） | [arXiv:2601.13671](https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1) |
| AOP 框架 (2024/10) | 可解性、完整性、非冗餘性三原則 | [arXiv:2410.02189](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2) |
| MetaGPT (ICLR 2024 Oral) | SOP 角色編碼，結構化文件通訊 | [arXiv:2308.00352](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352) |
| MegaAgent (ACL 2025) | 自主遞迴分解，590 agent 規模驗證 | [arXiv:2408.09955](https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955) |
| AgentOrchestra (2026/01) | TEA 協議，GAIA SOTA 89.04% | [arXiv:2506.12508](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508) |
| TDAG (2025) | DAG 結構 + 動態重規劃（注意：仍為順序執行） | [arXiv:2402.10178](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178) |
| DyLAN (COLM 2024) | 動態 agent 團隊選擇，mid-execution 淘汰低效 agent | [arXiv:2310.02170](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170) |
| DAG 分解與動態策略 (2024) | 顯式提及分解策略可依複雜度自適應，提升準確率 | [arXiv:2410.22457](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.22457v1) |
| ACONIC 限制式分解 (2025) | 以滿足性問題建模任務，樹寬衡量複雜度 | [arXiv:2510.07772](https://arxiv.org/html/2510.07772v1) |

# DeepSeek 近期進展 與 多Agent任務分解理論與實務

> 研究日期：2026-04-29

---

## 第一部分：DeepSeek 的真實改進機制

### 結論先說

**DeepSeek V3 / R1 的性能提升與「多Agent協作」無關。** 經查閱 DeepSeek 官方技術報告（arXiv:2412.19437、arXiv:2501.12948），未找到任何關於 agent swarm、多Agent訓練或多Agent推理的機制。「Agent群」的說法可能源自術語混淆。

### 實際改進機制（四大核心）

#### 1. 混合專家架構（MoE）— DeepSeek-V3
- 671B 總參數，每個 token 只激活 37B
- **auxiliary-loss-free 負載均衡**：相較過去 MoE 的新創，不再需要輔助損失函數
- **多 Token 預測（Multi-Token Prediction）**：同時預測多個未來 token，強化表示學習

> 注意：MoE 的「expert」聽起來像 agent，實際上是**單一模型內的子網路路由層**，非多Agent協作。

#### 2. 多頭潛在注意力（MLA）— DeepSeek-V2 & V3
- 將 KV cache 壓縮進低秩潛在空間
- 有效批次容量提升約 **9.6 倍**（相比標準多頭注意力）
- 純矩陣壓縮技術，與 agent 無關

#### 3. 硬體-演算法協同設計 — DeepSeek-V3
- **FP8 混合精度訓練**：首次在兆參數規模驗證，FLOPS 比 FP16 多一倍
- **DualPipe 演算法**：新型管線並行排程，重疊計算與通訊，減少空泡（pipeline bubble）
- **PTX 級 GPU 優化**：warp 層級專化，最大化 H800 利用率
- 全訓練成本：14.8T tokens，僅用 **278.8 萬 H800 GPU 小時**，且無 loss spike

#### 4. 群組相對策略優化（GRPO）— DeepSeek-R1
- 每個 prompt 生成**一組候選輸出**（group of outputs），使用當前 policy 採樣
- 以規則型 reward（正確性 + 格式）評分
- 以**組內平均作為 baseline** 計算相對優勢，取代 PPO 中獨立的 critic/value 網路
- 結果：單一模型通過純 RL 自發產生自我反思、回溯（「aha moments」）、動態推理長度

> 「GRPO 的 group」= 同一模型的一批採樣輸出，**不是多個獨立 agent**

### 術語混淆來源

| 混淆點 | 實際是什麼 |
|---|---|
| MoE 的「experts」 | 單一模型內的子網路，token 路由選擇 |
| GRPO 的「group」 | 同一模型一次採樣的多個候選答案 |
| DeepSeek 做「agent產品」 | Bloomberg 2025/09 報導 DeepSeek 正在**開發面向用戶的 agent 產品**（非訓練機制） |
| Kimi K2.6「Agent Swarm」 | 競爭對手 Kimi 的功能，不是 DeepSeek |
| 第三方整合 | CrewAI、MetaGPT 等**使用** DeepSeek 模型作為組件，不是 DeepSeek 自身架構 |

### 參考文獻
- [DeepSeek-R1 論文 (arXiv:2501.12948)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12948)
- [DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report (arXiv:2412.19437)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19437)
- [Martin Fowler DeepSeek 技術綜覽](https://martinfowler.com/articles/deepseek-papers.html)

---

## 第二部分：多 Agent 任務分解的理論與實務

### 背景：為什麼需要任務分解？

當單一 agent 面臨以下情況時，多 agent 系統才有必要：
- prompt 長度或複雜度超過 context window
- 任務需要不同專業工具組合
- 需要平行加速（獨立子任務可同時進行）
- 安全邊界要求（不同 agent 有不同權限）

**不要過早引入多 agent**：先用單一 agent + 工具，當單 agent 明顯失敗時再分解。

---

### 任務分解的三大正確性原則（AOP Framework）

來源：[Agent-Oriented Planning (arXiv:2410.02189)](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2)

1. **可解性（Solvability）**：每個子任務必須有至少一個 agent 能獨立完整解決。生成無法被任何 agent 解決的子任務是最主要的失敗原因。

2. **完整性（Completeness）**：所有子任務的聯集必須覆蓋原始問題。分解過程中的資訊遺失會使最終答案不完整。

3. **非冗餘性（Non-Redundancy）**：子任務不可重疊。重疊導致 agent 重複勞動，產生衝突輸出難以聚合。

> 違反以上三原則的「agent 大雜燴」系統，錯誤放大可達 **17 倍**。  
> 來源：[Why Your Multi-Agent System is Failing](https://towardsdatascience.com/why-your-multi-agent-system-is-failing-escaping-the-17x-error-trap-of-the-bag-of-agents/)

---

### 分解策略類型

| 策略 | 說明 | 適用時機 |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM 驅動（zero-shot）** | Orchestrator LLM 自由生成任務拆分 | 新穎、多變的任務 |
| **LLM 驅動（few-shot）** | 示例引導拆分格式 | 輸出結構有要求時 |
| **程式化 / SOP** | 固定邏輯拆分已知任務類型 | 重複、可預測的流程 |
| **HTN（層次任務網路）** | 抽象目標逐步精化為可執行步驟 | 含可重用子程序的複雜流程 |
| **DAG 動態生成** | 任務建模為帶依賴邊的節點圖，執行時生成 | 有明確前置條件的混合串/並行任務 |
| **限制式分解（ACONIC）** | 將任務建模為可滿足性問題；樹寬衡量複雜度 | 需要正確性保證的形式推理任務 |

---

### 執行拓撲類型

| 拓撲 | 結構 | 是否並行 | 控制方式 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **順序 / Pipeline** | 固定線性階段，每階段基於前一階段 | 否 | 確定性 |
| **並發 / Fan-out** | 同一輸入分發給多個 agent 同時執行 | 是 | Scatter-Gather |
| **層次（Hierarchical）** | 督導者與工作者組成的樹狀結構 | 層內部分並行 | 集中式 |
| **Handoff / 路由** | 控制動態轉交給更合適的 agent | 否（單一活躍） | 動態 |
| **Swarm** | 去中心化，共享狀態，無中央協調者 | 是 | 湧現式 |
| **Mesh** | 小型團隊內的明確 Peer-to-Peer 連接 | 部分 | 點對點 |
| **Magentic / 任務台帳** | Manager 動態建立與修正任務列表 | 部分 | 自適應 |

---

### 拓撲選擇決策表（Microsoft Azure，2026）

| 使用情況 | 推薦模式 |
|---|---|
| 明確線性依賴，無並行可能 | Sequential |
| 同一輸入的獨立分析，對延遲敏感 | Concurrent |
| 共識構建、迭代驗證（腦力激盪） | Group Chat |
| 開始時不知道需要哪個專家 | Handoff |
| 開放式、無預先解決路徑 | Magentic（任務台帳） |

來源：[Azure AI Agent Design Patterns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns)

---

### Anthropic 生產系統的實際數據

來源：[Anthropic 多 Agent 研究系統部落格](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system)

使用 Opus 4 作為 Orchestrator + Sonnet 4 作為 subagent，性能超越單一 Opus 4 達 **90.2%**。

| 複雜研究任務 | 10+ 個 | 每個 15+ |

1. 具體、明確的目標
2. 明確的任務邊界（**不**調查什麼）
3. 要求的輸出格式
4. 使用哪些工具及何時停止的指引

**最主要的失敗模式**：模糊的分解（如「研究半導體短缺」）導致 agent 重複搜尋相同內容。

---

#### LangGraph（LangChain）

- **注意**：並行失敗是原子性的——一個分支失敗，整個 superstep 的所有分支都失敗
- **實測加速**：137x（61s → 0.45s）
- 文件：https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/tutorials/multi_agent/hierarchical_agent_teams/

#### AutoGen（Microsoft）
- **分解策略四種模式**：(1) 函數式規劃器生成 3-5 子任務，(2) group chat 自動發言選擇，(3) group chat 自定確定性路由，(4) AutoBuild 動態生成 agent
- **角色類型**：`AssistantAgent`、`UserProxyAgent`、`ToolAgent`、`GroupChatManager`

#### MetaGPT
- **分解策略**：SOP 編碼，模擬軟體公司角色：PM → Architect → Project Manager → Engineer → QA
- **通訊**：傳遞**結構化文件**（PRD、系統設計、任務列表、程式碼），而非自然語言對話
- **發佈訂閱**：共享訊息池，agent 只消費相關訊息類型
- **性能**：Pass@1 85.9–87.7%（HumanEval）
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352

#### MegaAgent（ACL 2025）
- **分解策略**：全自主、無 SOP 的遞迴分解。Boss → Admin → Ordinary，admin 按需招募子 agent
- **時間複雜度**：O(log n) vs. 順序的 O(n)
- **依賴管理**：組內 chat（協作）+ 組間 chat via admin（跨組依賴）
- **規模**：最多 590 個 agent 的國家政策模擬
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955

#### AgentOrchestra（arXiv:2506.12508，GAIA SOTA 89.04%）
- **分解策略**：Planning agent + TEA 協議。todo 工具追蹤每個子任務（id、描述、參數、優先級、狀態、結果）
- **四種專業 subagent**：Deep Researcher（網路搜尋）、Browser Use Agent（網頁互動）、Deep Analyzer（多模態推理）、Tool Manager（動態建立新工具）
- **聚合**：Planning agent 匯總回饋，動態更新計劃，呼叫 done 工具標示完成
- 論文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508

---

### 依賴管理的五種模式

| 模式 | 說明 | 使用框架 |
|---|---|---|
| **順序閘控** | B 只在 A 的輸出落入共享狀態後才啟動 | LangGraph reducers, CrewAI context |
| **動態重規劃** | 每個子任務完成後根據實際結果修正剩餘任務 | TDAG, AgentOrchestra, MegaAgent |
| **發佈-訂閱** | Agent 訂閱相關訊息類型，只消費所需 | MetaGPT 共享訊息池 |
| **Contract Net** | Orchestrator 廣播任務，agent 競標，贏家被指派 | 古典 HMAS 方法 |
| **黑板 / 共享狀態** | 所有 agent 讀寫同一資料結構，Reducer 解衝突 | LangGraph state, Swarm context |

---

### 結果聚合的七種模式

| 聚合方法 | 最適用 | 使用在 |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM 合成** | 研究、分析、開放式任務 | Anthropic 系統, AgentOrchestra |
| **投票 / 多數決** | 分類、二元決策 | DyLAN, Group Chat |
| **加權合併** | 帶分數的推薦 | 含品質權重的並發協調 |
| **結構化接力** | 線性管線，前一輸出為下一輸入 | CrewAI sequential, MetaGPT |
| **Pydantic 驗證** | 可靠的結構化輸出 | CrewAI `output_pydantic`, AutoGen reflection |
| **層次融合** | 大規模層次系統 | MegaAgent, HMAS |
| **黑板整合** | 鬆耦合團隊 | Swarm 架構 |

---

### 主要失敗模式

1. **Agent 大雜燴（Bag of Agents）**：無結構拓撲 → 錯誤放大 17 倍
2. **模糊分解**：子任務沒有 agent 能解決（違反可解性）
3. **完整性缺口**：分解丟失原始問題資訊
4. **冗餘子任務**：多個 agent 調查同一角度，產生衝突答案
5. **Context Window 瓶頸**：Orchestrator-Worker 超過 50 個子結果時，orchestrator 成為限制
6. **原子性失敗傳播**：LangGraph 並行 superstep 一個分支失敗，全部失敗
7. **層次延遲堆疊**：每層至少 2-3 秒，4 層層次系統有 10 秒基礎延遲

---

### 五種模式的決策矩陣（拓撲選擇）

| 任務特性 | 使用模式 | 注意事項 |
|---|---|---|
| 獨立子任務，無需跨 agent 通訊 | Orchestrator-Worker | 超過 50 個結果時 orchestrator context 瓶頸 |
| 有清晰階段邊界的固定序列 | Pipeline | 5 個階段最少 10+ 秒 |
| 3–8 個緊密協作者迭代共享成果 | Mesh | 超過 8 個 agent 組合爆炸 |
| 大型未知問題空間需要探索 | Swarm | 可觀察性差，難以強制排序 |
| 20+ agent 的多領域企業規模 | Hierarchical | 每層最少 2-3 秒延遲 |

---

## 學術調查論文（2024-2026 精選）

| 論文 | 核心貢獻 | 連結 |
|---|---|---|
| LLM Multi-Agent Survey (IJCAI 2024) | 三種推理框架分類：多階段串行、集體決策、自我精煉 | [arXiv:2402.01680](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01680) |
| LLM-MAS 近期進展 (2025/01) | 分類應用：任務解決、模擬、評估；指出依賴管理仍未解決 | [arXiv:2412.17481](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.17481) |
| LLM Multi-Agent 挑戰 (2025/05) | 四大對齊挑戰；7 種單 agent 分解方法分類 | [arXiv:2402.03578](https://arxiv.org/html/2402.03578v2) |
| HMAS 分類學 (2025) | 五軸特徵化 + 六種協調機制 | [arXiv:2508.12683](https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12683) |
| 多 Agent 協調架構與協議 (2026/01) | 三層架構：專業 agent + 協調層 + 通訊協議（MCP+A2A） | [arXiv:2601.13671](https://arxiv.org/html/2601.13671v1) |
| AOP 框架 (2024/10) | 可解性、完整性、非冗餘性三原則 | [arXiv:2410.02189](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02189v2) |
| MetaGPT (ICLR 2024 Oral) | SOP 角色編碼，結構化文件通訊 | [arXiv:2308.00352](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352) |
| MegaAgent (ACL 2025) | 自主遞迴分解，590 agent 規模驗證 | [arXiv:2408.09955](https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09955) |
| AgentOrchestra (2026/01) | TEA 協議，GAIA SOTA 89.04% | [arXiv:2506.12508](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12508) |
| TDAG (2025) | DAG 結構 + 動態重規劃（注意：仍為順序執行） | [arXiv:2402.10178](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10178) |
| DyLAN (COLM 2024) | 動態 agent 團隊選擇，mid-execution 淘汰低效 agent | [arXiv:2310.02170](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02170) |
| DAG 分解與動態策略 (2024) | 顯式提及分解策略可依複雜度自適應，提升準確率 | [arXiv:2410.22457](https://arxiv.org/html/2410.22457v1) |
| ACONIC 限制式分解 (2025) | 以滿足性問題建模任務，樹寬衡量複雜度 | [arXiv:2510.07772](https://arxiv.org/html/2510.07772v1) |

content.text:
文檔已建立：`doc/20260429_deepseek_multiagent_task_decomposition.md`

---

## 研究摘要

**關於 DeepSeek**：沒有找到 DeepSeek 使用 agent 群的證據，「agent群」的說法是**術語混淆**。DeepSeek 的改進來自四個單一模型技術：
- **MoE**（混合專家架構）— 671B 參數但每次只激活 37B
- **MLA**（多頭潛在注意力）— KV cache 壓縮，批次量提升 9.6 倍
- **GRPO**（群組相對策略優化）— 單一模型自我競爭的 RL 訓練，非多 agent
- **FP8 + DualPipe**（硬體-演算法協同設計）— 訓練成本大幅降低

**你最想知道的：多 Agent 任務分解**，文檔整理了完整的理論與實務，重點摘要：

1. **三大正確性原則（AOP）**：可解性、完整性、非冗餘性 — 違反則錯誤放大 17 倍
2. **選拓撲的決策表**：獨立子任務用 Orchestrator-Worker，固定流程用 Pipeline，探索型用 Swarm
3. **Anthropic 生產數據**：每個 subagent 的 brief 必須包含「明確不做什麼」，否則 agent 互相重複搜尋
4. **五種依賴管理模式**、**七種結果聚合模式** — 文檔都有對應表格
5. **推薦重點論文**：AgentOrchestra（GAIA SOTA 89%）、MegaAgent（590 agent 規模驗證）、HMAS 分類學
